categories.title: 🎬 Movie Star
categories.description: Create a funny story about becoming a famous movie star!

stories.template: I was having one of those completely ordinary **{adj1}** Tuesday afternoons when suddenly a stretch limousine pulled up beside me with tires screeching. The window rolled down smoothly, and there sat **{celebrity}**, wearing sunglasses that probably cost more than my entire existence. They pointed directly at me and declared, "You! Yes, YOU! I've been searching for someone with such a **{adj2}** **{bodypart}**!" Before I could process what was happening, I was being pulled into the leather interior. **{celebrity}** explained that their latest blockbuster needed someone who could authentically portray a person who had just discovered their pet **{animal}** could talk. The next **{number}** weeks were a whirlwind. I found myself **{verb1}** in front of massive camera rigs, surrounded by crew members who took their jobs very seriously despite the absurdity. Between takes, craft services served nothing but **{food}**, which I was contractually obligated to eat on camera. By the time filming wrapped, I had gained fifteen pounds and developed a strange appreciation for **{food}**. The premiere was surreal - flashing cameras, screaming fans, and **{celebrity}** keeping an arm around me. Sometimes I still pinch myself, but the Oscar nomination on my desk reminds me this is actually my life now.

The whole thing started because I decided to treat myself to a quiet afternoon at **{place}**, enjoying what I thought would be a completely anonymous experience. I was just sitting there, **{verb1}** in my own thoughts, when I noticed people starting to whisper and point. That's when **{celebrity}** approached my table, looking absolutely **{adj1}** and slightly out of breath. "I've been looking everywhere!" they exclaimed, grabbing my **{bodypart}** with both hands. "The casting director said you were perfect, but I didn't believe it until now. We start shooting tomorrow!" Around us, other patrons had pulled out their phones. Before I could formulate a response, **{celebrity}** had already pulled out a contract. "**{number}** million dollars, three movie deal," they said casually. My hand moved almost involuntarily, signing away my next several years. "**{exclaim}**!" I finally managed to say. They hugged me before disappearing as quickly as they had arrived, leaving me with a life-changing contract and no idea what I had just agreed to.

Walking into that **{movie}** movie audition, I felt **{adj1}** and completely unprepared. The waiting room was filled with people who looked like they had been born to be on screen. When they called my name, I walked in trying to project confidence I definitely did not feel. **{celebrity}** was sitting behind a long table with three other people. They asked me to perform a scene that involved losing control of my **{bodypart}** in a dramatic fashion. I **{verb1}** so hard that I actually lost my balance and fell off the stool. The room went silent. But then **{celebrity}** started laughing, not meanly, but in genuine amusement. "That's it! That's exactly the energy we need!" The others started nodding, scribbling notes. "**{exclaim}**!" I said, still on the floor. They offered me the role on the spot. **{number}** months later, I'm watching myself on the big screen, amazed that falling on my face literally launched my career.

Everyone had been telling me for years that I looked like **{celebrity}**, but I never really saw it myself. That all changed on a **{adj1}** morning when I decided to treat myself to breakfast at **{place}**. I was sitting there, **{verb1}** through an absurdly expensive omelet, when I noticed a woman at the next table keeping glancing in my direction. Then she stood up, walked over, and asked if I was **{celebrity}**. When I said no, she pulled out her phone and started taking pictures. Within minutes, the restaurant was buzzing. Other patrons asked for selfies, someone brought me a napkin to sign, and the manager offered complimentary dessert. By the end, I had **{number}** new followers and three talent agency cards. My **{adj2}** doppelgänger experience had accidentally launched me into a career as a lookalike. I still go to **{place}** sometimes. They always recognize me.

I was just **{verb1}** around **{place}** on a lazy Sunday when my phone rang with an unknown number. On the other end was **{celebrity}**, sounding extremely excited. "I saw your video! The one with the **{adj1}** **{bodypart}** thing! You're perfect for my new project!" Before I could ask questions, they hung up. I spent the night searching my social media, finally finding a joke video that had gone viral without me noticing. The next morning, I showed up at their office. **{celebrity}** was waiting in the lobby and ushered me into a meeting where **{number}** people waited with contracts. "**{exclaim}**!" I said when they told me what they wanted. Six months later, that silly video had transformed my entire life.

The casting call seemed innocent - they wanted people willing to eat **{food1}** on camera. I showed up at **{place}** feeling **{adj1}**. The waiting room was filled with hopeful contestants. When they called my name, **{celebrity}** was standing behind a kitchen counter in a chef's apron. They explained this was about eating with passion, with emotion, with your entire soul. I took my first bite and my reaction was so genuine that **{celebrity}** immediately **{verb1}** across the set, grabbed my **{bodypart}**, and declared me the winner. "**{number}** seasons and I've never seen a reaction like that!" they announced. I went from unemployed to television star in one lunch, all because of my authentic appreciation for **{food1}**.

categories.title: 🍕 Restaurant
categories.description: A dining experience you'll never forget!

stories.template: The restaurant had been recommended by exactly no one, but something about its **{adj1}** exterior drew me in. I settled into a corner table and ordered the **{food1}**. When it arrived, the dish was **{verb1}** right there on the plate, moving in ways food should not move. I called the waiter over, pointing at my **{bodypart}** which had started shaking. "**{exclaim}**!" I exclaimed. The waiter smiled and explained this was the restaurant's signature "dining theater" concept. Apparently **{celebrity}** had been here **{number}** times and loved it. They brought me complimentary **{liquid}** to calm my nerves. By the end, I was laughing so hard that other diners started clapping. I left with a story I still tell at every dinner party.

I had been planning this dinner at **{place}** for months. The reservation system was notoriously difficult - call exactly three months ahead at 9 AM. But I persisted. I ordered the chef's signature **{food1}**. The waiter brought it with theatrical flair. But halfway through my first bite, something felt wrong. My **{bodypart}** started tingling, then burning, then I was **{verb1}** so hard I nearly fell off my chair. The entire restaurant went silent as **{number}** diners watched my public struggle. "**{exclaim}**!" I gasped. The chef emerged and explained they had accidentally used a special pepper from a remote region. They comped my meal and gave me cooling sauce. I still have nightmares about that **{food1}**. But would I go back? Absolutely.

The food critic assignment was supposed to be straightforward. But nothing about this place was straightforward. From the moment I walked in, everything felt **{adj1}** and **{adj2}**. I ordered the **{food1}**, which the waiter described as "a journey through the chef's emotional landscape." Halfway through, I felt something touch my **{bodypart}** under the table. I **{verb1}** in surprise. It was **{celebrity}** at the next table, who had been trying to get my attention. We talked for **{number}** hours after closing. The review I wrote became my most popular piece ever. Sometimes the best stories come from unexpected detours.

The street food tour at **{place}** was supposed to be educational. The **{adj1}** **{food1}** looked innocent. The vendor handed it with a knowing smile I should have recognized as a warning. I took a big bite, and immediately my **{bodypart}** felt like fire. I was **{verb1}** around, desperately searching for water. "**{exclaim}**!" I screamed. The guide explained this dish was called "the truth teller" - so spicy people say whatever comes to mind. For **{number}** minutes, I confessed secrets I'd been keeping for years. The photos still circulate in my friend group. I learned two things: never underestimate street food, and always carry water.

The all-you-can-eat challenge at **{place}** seemed like a great idea after drinks with friends. The **{adj1}** **{food1}** was piled high on plates that kept coming. Halfway through, my **{bodypart}** sent clear signals I had made a terrible mistake. But I was too far in to quit. I **{verb1}** another bite, operating on pure stubbornness. "**{exclaim}**!" I groaned as I set down my fork. The restaurant erupted. I had eaten **{number}** pounds, broken the house record, and won a year of free meals. The problem? I couldn't move for six hours. My friends wheeled me out in a kitchen cart. The photos are humiliating and oddly proud.

The dinner theater at **{place}** promised culinary delights with live performance. The venue was **{adj1}** and **{adj2}**, tables in a circle around a stage. One option: the chef's mystery **{food1}**. As the show progressed, actors interacted with diners. One approached my table, looked at my **{bodypart}**, and incorporated it into their monologue. I was **{verb1}** so hard I nearly knocked over my water. The performance continued for **{number}** hours, each course timed to plot developments. By dessert, we were all invested in both story and meal. The final scene had everyone standing, applauding, some with tears. Most unique dining experience of my life.

categories.title: 🎢 Theme Park
categories.description: The most extreme theme park adventure!

stories.template: The theme park had been hyped as the most **{adj1}** amusement destination on the west coast. I arrived at opening time, fueled by **{number}** **{food1}**s and an unhealthy amount of excitement. The crowds were massive. My first target was the new roller coaster that everyone said was so **{adj2}** it had made **{celebrity}** cry during their promotional ride. As I boarded, I felt my **{bodypart}** start to shake with fear and anticipation. The safety bar clicked into place, and we began **{verb1}** up the first hill, higher and higher until the entire park stretched below us. Then came the drop - a stomach-lurching plunge that had everyone screaming "**{exclaim}**!" Mid-ride, somehow, a costumed **{animal}** mascot appeared to be running alongside the track, waving at us. I don't know how it got there. By the time we returned to the station, my legs were jelly, my voice was gone, and I immediately got back in line. Best day ever.

Working at **{place}** during summer break was supposed to be easy money. What I didn't anticipate was how **{adj1}** the job would be during peak season. My favorite part was getting to experience the **{ride}** whenever I wanted. During slow periods, I would hop on, ride it empty, and feel like I had the entire park to myself. One afternoon, I was **{verb1}** through the pre-ride safety check when I noticed my **{bodypart}** tingling in a way that had nothing to do with the ride's usual sensations. Turns out there had been a minor electrical issue, and I had unknowingly ridden through it **{number}** times. "**{exclaim}**!" my supervisor yelled when they caught me. I was fine, but they shut down the ride. The incident earned me a permanent ban from that attraction, but the story was worth more than any shift differential.

The eating contest before the rides was a decision I would regret. The **{adj1}** **{food1}** challenge seemed manageable. I managed to consume **{number}** servings, which felt like a victory until I stood up and immediately felt my **{bodypart}** protest. My friends convinced me to get on the **{adj2}** roller coaster anyway. "It'll help settle your stomach!" they insisted, which is absolutely not how digestion works. Halfway through the first loop, I **{verb1}** in a way that changed the lives of everyone in the front two rows. "**{exclaim}**!" screamed the person behind me. I was escorted off with a lifetime ban from that attraction. The photos look worse than reality. Sometimes the best stories come from the worst decisions.

The urban legend had circulated for years - that the old **{ride}** at the abandoned park would sometimes operate on its own. I didn't believe it until my friends convinced me to sneak in one night. The park was **{adj1}** in the moonlight, all the rides casting long shadows. We made our way to the ride, and that's when things got strange. My **{bodypart}** started tingling as we approached. Suddenly, the ride lit up and started **{verb1}** on its own. We stood frozen, watching it complete a full cycle. Then we heard it - the sound of an **{animal}**, somewhere in the darkness. "**{exclaim}**!" one of my friends whispered, and we all ran without looking back. We never went back. None of us will talk about what we saw.

The VIP experience at **{place}** cost more than my monthly rent, but when **{celebrity}** invited me for a private tour, how could I say no? The park was **{adj1}** and empty, all regular guests cleared out for our exclusive access. **{celebrity}** led me from ride to ride, laughing like a kid. At one point, they grabbed my **{bodypart}** and **{verb1}** me onto the fastest roller coaster before I could process what was happening. We rode it **{number}** times in a row, screaming our heads off, completely free from the usual constraints of fame. "**{exclaim}**!" **{celebrity}** yelled during the final drop. I realized that despite all their success, they were just a person looking for genuine connection. We took a photo I still have framed. Sometimes the most extraordinary experiences come from unexpected friendships.

The petting zoo at **{place}** seemed like a harmless addition to our theme park day. The animals looked **{adj1}** and well-cared-for. That's when the **{animal}** locked eyes with me. There was something in its gaze I can only describe as calculated malice. Before I could react, it had targeted my **{bodypart}** and was **{verb1}** toward me with alarming speed. I tried to back away, but I was trapped against the fence. "**{exclaim}**!" I screamed as the creature made contact. **{number}** zookeepers rushed over, trying to separate us. The photos they took for the incident report still make me laugh. I left with scratches, a tetanus shot, and a story that has become legendary. The park gave me lifetime passes as compensation, which I use exclusively to avoid the petting zoo.

The vacation to **{place}** had been planned for months - carefully researched hotels, meticulously organized itineraries. I had envisioned peaceful days on pristine beaches. What I got was an education in things going wrong. Day one started with a **{adj1}** encounter at the airport when I realized I had packed my sunscreen in luggage that was sent to a different continent. By day two, I was attempting to **{verb1}** on the beach using a stolen hotel towel as shade, when a wild **{animal}** decided my belongings looked appealing. The creature grabbed my bag and ran, and I chased it using only my **{bodypart}** to propel myself through sand. Exhausted, I stopped at a roadside stand and ordered **{food1}**. "**{exclaim}**!" I exclaimed after the first bite. The meal cost **{number}** times what it should have. I went home sunburned with no souvenirs, but a story that became legendary in my family.

The all-inclusive resort at **{place}** promised paradise. The photos online looked **{adj1}** and inviting. What they didn't show was the construction on three sides, the algae-covered beach, or that "unlimited" drinks required extra fees. My **{bodypart}** started reacting to something in the pool within hours, leaving me **{verb1}** around the resort in search of relief. To make matters worse, **{celebrity}** was staying there and complained about my appearance in their Instagram stories. **{number}** people recognized me and asked for their autograph instead of mine. "**{exclaim}**!" I finally yelled at the front desk. They offered a discount on a future stay. I declined. Some lessons are learned through suffering, and I am now very well educated about reading resort reviews.

The vacation rental looked **{adj1}** in the photos - modern furnishings, stunning views. The reality was **{adj2}** and nothing like the listing. The "stunning views" overlooked a parking garage. The "modern furnishings" were broken. I tried to sleep, but something kept touching my **{bodypart}** under the covers. When I turned on the light and **{verb1}** at what I found, I knew I couldn't stay. "**{exclaim}**!" I screamed, packing my bags at **{number}** AM. The host was unresponsive, the booking platform's customer service was closed, and I was stranded. I ended up at an airport hotel, sleeping in my clothes, vowing to never trust professionally shot photos again. Some travel experiences teach you to appreciate home.

The culinary tour of **{place}** was supposed to be the highlight of my trip. The **{adj1}** **{food1}** was the third stop, and by all accounts, it was the masterpiece. The vendor prepared it with obvious pride. I took a reverent first bite, and immediately my **{bodypart}** started sending distress signals. I was **{verb1}** around the market within minutes, searching for water, for relief. "**{exclaim}**!" I gasped, drawing concerned looks. The tour guide rushed over with a remedy that worked within seconds, then explained the dish was notoriously potent for visitors. **{number}** other tourists had the same reaction that week. I finished the tour with newfound respect for local cuisine and a souvenir bottle of the remedy. Sometimes the best memories come with a side of suffering.

Losing my passport was the first mistake of what would become a **{adj1}** journey through international bureaucracy. I spent **{number}** hours at the embassy, my **{bodypart}** aching from hard chairs. They made me **{verb1}** my entire travel history, document every interaction, and provide evidence of identity I did not have. "**{exclaim}**!" I finally exclaimed when they told me the temporary document would take three days. Three days! My carefully planned itinerary for **{place}** was falling apart. I ended up spending those days wandering the city near the embassy, discovering neighborhoods I would never have visited otherwise. When I finally received my documents, I realized that sometimes the best travel experiences are the ones you never planned.

The Instagram photos had made **{place}** look absolutely **{adj1}** - crystal clear water, perfect weather, people effortlessly **{verb1}**. I didn't consider that photos might be curated and filtered. I arrived to find crowds all trying to take the same photo, the water murky, the weather uncooperative. But I was determined to get my shot. I waded in, positioned myself, and immediately felt my **{bodypart}** cramp from the cold. "**{exclaim}**!" I screamed, unable to move, unable to get back to shore. **{number}** tourists pulled out phones to record my distress instead of helping. I eventually made it back, shivering and humiliated, but the photo I got - the one where I'm clearly in pain - got more likes than anything I've ever posted. Social media is strange.

The arena was packed to capacity. The **{genre}** concert of the decade was finally happening. The energy in the building was **{adj1}**, electric. When the lights finally went down and the first notes hit, I lost all control. I was **{verb1}** so hard that I accidentally elbowed the person next to me with my **{bodypart}**. They turned around, and I froze - it was **{celebrity}**, incognito in a baseball cap that fooled absolutely no one. "Sorry!" I yelled over the music, mortified. They just laughed, handed me their half-eaten **{food1}**, and started singing along. We spent the next **{number}** hours as unlikely concert buddies, sharing food, singing at the top of our lungs. "**{exclaim}**!" we both screamed during the final song, two strangers united by music. We exchanged numbers afterward. Still friends today.

Front row. Center stage. The **{band}** concert that would define the rest of my social existence. I had camped out for **{number}** hours to secure this spot. As the house lights dimmed and the crowd began to roar, I knew every sacrifice had been worth it. The lead singer scanned the front row, and somehow, their eyes locked onto mine. They pointed directly at my **{bodypart}** - I was wearing a particularly **{adj1}** shirt - and yelled "YOU! Come on stage!" I **{verb1}** in absolute panic as security escorted me up. "**{exclaim}**!" the crowd screamed. I played **{number}** songs with them, my voice cracking, my hands shaking, living every music fan's dream. The videos have millions of views now. I still get recognized at concerts. Sometimes the craziest risks lead to the best rewards.

The **{genre}** festival had been the talk of my friend group for months. I had the tickets, the outfit, the entire aesthetic. What I didn't have was a legitimate way to get in - I had missed the age requirement by two months. The fake ID looked **{adj1}** enough, I thought. But as I approached security, they started looking **{adj2}**, examining my ID with far more scrutiny. I started **{verb1}** nervously, clutching my **{bodypart}** where I had hidden the backup ID. "**{exclaim}**!" they yelled, spotting the discrepancy. I ran, weaving through **{number}** different stages before finally losing them. I spent the rest of the festival looking over my shoulder, but the music was incredible. Got kicked out on day two. Still worth it.

The underground venue at **{place}** was the kind of place you only heard about through word of mouth. Getting in required knowing someone who knew someone. We climbed through a window because the line was too long, and I landed wrong, immediately feeling something in my **{bodypart}** protest. But the music had already started. I **{verb1}** through the pain, pushing forward into the crowd. "**{exclaim}**!" the band yelled when they saw blood dripping down my leg. They stopped mid-song, security was called, and suddenly I was the center of attention. **{number}** people offered to help, someone brought ice, and the band dedicated their next song to "the kid who bled for music." I still have the stained jeans. They're hanging in my closet as a trophy.

I had never been into **{genre}** music - not that I disliked it, I just hadn't given it a chance. But my friends were obsessed, so I agreed to go. The crowd was **{adj1}**, buzzing with energy I had never experienced. When the music started, everyone began **{verb1}**, and I was immediately swept up. I lost a shoe within minutes, scraped my **{bodypart}** on something I still can't identify, and somehow ended up in the very center of the mosh pit. "**{exclaim}**!" I screamed, equal parts terror and exhilaration. By the end, I had bought **{number}** merch items I didn't need, made friends with strangers, and developed a genuine love for a genre I had previously dismissed. Sometimes the best experiences are the ones you least expect.

The street performer at **{place}** looked like any other musician trying to make a few dollars. But something about their presence made me stop. They started playing, and I was immediately transfixed. My **{bodypart}** started tapping, then my whole body was moving, and before I knew it, I was **{verb1}** in the middle of the sidewalk, completely losing myself. **{number}** people formed a circle, filming, cheering, throwing money. "**{exclaim}**!" I yelled when the performer invited me to sing along. The video went viral - apparently my enthusiastic but tone-deaf contribution was exactly what the internet needed. I now have a small but dedicated following. The performer and I still play together sometimes. Life is strange.

categories.title: 😈 The Hookup
categories.description: A wild night out that got completely out of hand... (18+)

stories.template: The bar was crowded and loud, the kind of place where you have to lean in close to be heard. That's where I first noticed them - standing at the far end, looking absolutely **{adj1}**. They caught me staring and smiled. We talked for maybe twenty minutes, but it felt like both an eternity and no time at all. Their hand found my **{bodypart1}** under the bar, and I responded by grazing their **{bodypart2}**. The conversation dried up, replaced by mutual understanding. "My place or yours?" they asked. We ended up at **{place}**, and things escalated quickly. They started **{verb1}** in ways that made me lose all coherent thought. "**{exclaim}**!" I gasped, probably louder than intended. We went at it for **{number}** hours, losing track of time. Morning came too soon. They left without waking me, just a note with their number. We never did call. Some nights are meant to remain singular.

The work conference at **{place}** was exactly as exciting as you'd expect - endless presentations, lukewarm coffee. But the after-party was where things got interesting. The hotel bar was packed with attendees from every company. That's where I met them - someone from a competing firm, **{adj1}** and charming in a way that felt dangerous. We talked about everything except work. Their hand found my **{bodypart}** under the table, and I didn't pull away. "Room?" they asked. I nodded. What followed was **{number}** hours of the most intense connection. "**{exclaim}**!" I gasped at one point, loud enough to worry about neighbors. They **{verb1}** me in ways that made me forget my own name. Morning came with awkward small talk and business cards we both knew we'd never use. We ran into each other at next year's conference. Didn't mention it. Still think about it though.

The friends-with-benefits arrangement was supposed to be simple - physical attraction, no complications. We had discussed the terms over drinks, both emerging from relationships that had been too serious. This would be casual. But then they started **{verb1}** me in ways that blurred every line we had drawn. Their **{bodypart1}** against my **{bodypart2}**, the intensity building until I couldn't remember what casual felt like. "**{exclaim}**!" I screamed, and the walls definitely heard us. **{number}** months of this, and somewhere along the way, I caught feelings. The **{adj1}** sex became **{adj2}** intimacy, and I knew we had a problem. They felt it too. We never talked about it. The arrangement ended when they moved cities. We still text sometimes. Both pretending we don't remember.

The party at **{place}** was the kind of event that exists in a gray area - not quite legal, not quite safe, but absolutely unmissable. The music was **{adj1}**, the crowd dense, the energy electric. I had come alone, which somehow made everything feel more dangerous. That's where I found them - in a corner, barely lit, their eyes locking onto mine. They gestured toward their **{bodypart}**, then toward the hallway. I followed without hesitation. We ended up in a room not meant for guests, **{verb1}** against a wall that vibrated with bass. "**{exclaim}**!" I gasped, too loud, not caring. Someone knocked on the door **{number}** times before we stopped. We ignored them. When we finally emerged, separately, we didn't acknowledge each other. But I saw them across the room later, and they smiled. We exchanged numbers. Still have it saved.

The dating app had been a joke between friends. I wasn't expecting much. Their profile was sparse, just a few photos and interesting bio. We matched. We talked. We agreed to meet. The bar was crowded, but I spotted them immediately. They were **{adj1}** in person. We talked for an hour, then two, then they asked if I wanted to continue somewhere more private. I said yes. Back at their place, their **{bodypart1}** found my **{bodypart2}** before the door was fully closed. We **{verb1}** against the wall, then the floor, then the bed. "**{exclaim}**!" I gasped **{number}** times that night. Morning came with sunlight and silence. They made coffee. We ate breakfast together. They walked me to the door. We never matched again. Sometimes one night is exactly enough.

The wedding was beautiful - the kind of elaborate affair where nobody knows anyone else, but everyone is united in celebration. I was there as a plus-one who had lost my plus-one to work. They were there alone, looking slightly uncomfortable in a tuxedo. We met at the bar. One drink became two, two became three, and somewhere around four, they **{verb1}** my **{bodypart}** under the table. The reception was in full swing, but we slipped away unnoticed. "**{place}**," they whispered, nodding toward an empty room. What followed was **{number}** minutes of adrenaline-fueled intimacy. "**{exclaim}**!" I gasped, trying to stay quiet. We returned to the reception separately, sat at different tables, never acknowledged each other again. But during the final dance, I caught their eye. They smiled. I smiled back. The end.

categories.title: 🍺 The Hangover
categories.description: Waking up after a night you can't remember...

stories.template: Consciousness returned slowly, like surfacing from deep water. My head was pounding with a **{adj1}** rhythm that made me immediately regret every decision. I opened my eyes and found myself in **{place}**, which was confusing because this was definitely not my apartment. My **{bodypart}** was throbbing, and I had a vague sense I had done something profoundly stupid. The last clear memory was doing shots of **{alcohol}** with friends, feeling invincible. I checked my phone. **{number}** missed calls. **{number}** texts. One video I was almost afraid to open. It showed me **{verb1}** on a table, yelling "**{exclaim}**!" while **{embarrass}**. The video had been viewed 47,000 times. My mother had commented. I was never drinking again. (I was drinking again the following weekend. Some lessons take longer to learn.)

The first thing I noticed was the tattoo. I was looking at it in the mirror, trying to understand when and why I had made this decision. It said "**{exclaim}**!" in script across my **{bodypart}**, and I had absolutely no memory of getting it. The skin was still tender, which meant it was recent. The second thing I noticed was that I was at **{place}**, which was not my home. The third thing was that I had been **{verb1}** for an extended period. I texted my friends: "What happened last night?" The response was immediate: "Legendary. You're never living it down." **{number}** of them sent videos. I watched none of them. I called the tattoo shop from the aftercare card on my nightstand. They confirmed I had been very insistent about the design. I'm keeping it. Some mistakes are too good to undo.

The bathtub was full of ice. This was not how I usually woke up. I was submerged up to my neck, my **{bodypart}** numb from the cold, my brain struggling to piece together how I had gotten here. The last thing I remembered was ordering **{alcohol}** at a bar, feeling **{adj1}** and invincible. Now I was here, **{verb1}** in a bathtub, my phone missing, my clothes nowhere in sight. "**{exclaim}**!" I screamed when I finally managed to stand. **{number}** voicemails from my boss. I listened to none of them. I found my clothes in the hallway, put them on wet, and left without saying goodbye. I was fired the next day. The story was worth losing the job. I tell it at parties.

Airport security was the last place I expected to wake up.
